questions.text: Does Store manager address safety at the morning assembly or monthly meeting etc?
Do you implement BS Safety fundamental Activities (3S, KY, RA and Safety rules) regularly?
Do you review site H&S performances results and take any actions against identified opportunities for improvements?
Does the Store Management regularly involve all teammates with safety discussions and incorporate their idea into safety action?
Are all teammates provided with PPEs needed for their tasks?
Are all teammates are involved with 3S activities?
Do you comfortable reporting safety issues to your store manager?
Do you know how to report incidents?
Are you aware of residual or high risks and inform teammates?
Are teammates corrected when not following Safety Rules? And are you leading by example?
Are you aware of required PPE to use?
Do you know how to report incidents?
Are teammates aware of recent safety incidents, if any.
Are you actively participating in safety discussions.
Have you reported unsafe conditions when identified?
Have you intervened to warn your teammate or reported when unsafe behavior is identified?
Teammates ensure step stools or ladders are in good condition before using.
Are machine, power tools and hand tools inspected before using to ensure they are in good condition.
Have teammates proposed safety improvements to prevent injuries or near misses.
Do you work safely even when busy?
Do all teammates participate in safety activities?
You allocate time for teammates to perform 3S activities.
Do you know how to access safety procedures and standards?
Do you know the Rules of Safety that are required to be followed?
You recognize Teammate when they practice safe behaviors.
What do you do for your safety when you encounter an abnormal situation?
Do you communicate safety messages to teammates to improve their safety awareness?
Do you feel comfortable stopping work if you feel there is a risk of injury.
Do you maintain the skill qualification list/table including vehicle elevation/lowering operation?
Routine Lift inspections are completed by outside vendor?
Are proper lifting equipment used (under load capacity) and are lifting arms set at appropriate position of vehicle?
What do you check to ensure the lift is adapted for the vehicle you need to service?
Do you observe teammates to ensure they follow lifting procedure?
Do you maintain the list of teammates who have legal licenses and/or legal training certifications required to perform the services including tire inflation, driving vehicle/forklift.
Are prevention measures taken to ensure no person, objects, or body parts are in the danger zone when inflating tires? (ex. Use safety gauge/safety net, Keep enough distance etc.)
How do you determine the inflation pressure needed for the tyres you are inflating?
Do you always inspect the tire and the rim for signs of deterioration before inflating?
Do you check frequently the tire during inflation and for what signs?
Are you aware of the proper lifting techniques?
Are paths kept clear to allow for material movement in the shop area?
What are the rules for storing tyres to avoid risk of fall or ergonomic risks?
Do teammates use mechanical aids when handling heavy objects (e.g. rim, oil drums) or tyre/wheel assembly or alternatively ask for assistance?
Are heavier items or frequently used items stored at mid-body height to make them easier to reach?
What is the rule to prevent the injury caused by overturning forklift? (question to teammate who may drive forklift)
Are vehicles parked at a location where the task can be conducted in a safe condition? (e.g. flat surface, wheel chocks, cones used, away from traffic).
What are the rules when driving a car for test drive and/or road service?
What are the rules applicable when guiding a vehicle in the store premises?
Do teammates complete an inspection to confirm safe operation of the vehicle before driving?
When you perform vehicle maintenance services, what do you do to prevent injury caused by rotating/moving part of vehicle?
Do Rotating equipment or objects under mechanical compression or tension have guarding/barriers? (e.g. tire balance MC, springs, shock absorber machine, brake lathes, bearing press, grinders, etc.).
Are the right tools used for specific tasks and in good condition?
Equipment that is unsafe and requires maintenance or repair is immediately taken out of service.
What do you do for your safety when you have any doubt during maintenance service on EV?
Does Leader to GENBA operation discuss Safety at regularly scheduled meetings?
Have Leader to GENBA operation communicated measurable expectations for safety performance?
Do you make effort in order that safety is aligned with other business values such as revenue, margin and cost reduction?
Do you make effort to allocate support and resources for implementing safety programs and activities?
Are supervisors and Leaders to GENBA operation trained to use "On the spot coaching" when a safety risk is identified?
Are Best Practices collected and used to improve the Safety Program?
Is the effectiveness of the Site Safety Rules reviewed after incidents and audits?
Does Leader to GENBA operation use leading & lagging metrics to guide continuous improvement efforts and decision-making?
Are safety goals reviewed/revised regularly and used in business planning?
Are responsibilities for Safety on each positions (Leaders to Genba operation, Supervisors and Teammates) defined?
Is Safety Orientation/Induction Training required for new Teammates? Can you describe the process?
Do Teammates receive training in a language and at a literacy level that all of them can understand?
Is there a formal safety training program for Teammates, including  supervisors and  Leaders to GENBA operation?
Are safety trends or analytics shared with Teammates?
Are Teammates informed of their personal responsibility for a safe workplace?
Are Teammates trained on how to report an injury, illness, hazard, or concern, including near misses?
Does Leader to GENBA operation seek Teammate input on hazard reduction and safety improvement?
Has the Bridgestone Safety Mission Statement been communicated to all Teammates?
Are cross-functional teams used to address specific safety and health concerns?
Is there a recognition program for teammates that demonstrate safe behaviors?
Are relevant Safety Procedures available to all Teammates?
Is Safety Training delivered by qualified trainers?
Are Near Miss and Lesson Learned Reports reviewed and used to improve the Safety Program?
Are teammates provided adequate safety equipment? Do they know how to use them?
Is there a an assessment program with scheduled compliance and systems audits?
Is the safety training curriculum tailored to the specific roles and responsibilities at each level of the organization?
Is a Risk Assessment conducted for new projects, materials and work methods?
Are certifications and training required by government regulations tracked to ensure completion?
Have important documents and records that need to be kept been identified? All documents that are legally required are kept.
Are regulatory and corporate compliance systematically checked to ensure they are up-to-date?
Is there an established emergency policy/procedure that complies with regulations?
Are hazards associated with emergencies and non-routine operations identified in the emergency action plan and operating procedures, respectively?
Are Emergency Plans written, communicated, revised, and facility drills conducted? Does the program update based on the result of the drill?
Is all regulatory required safety training included in the safety training matrix/plan?
Do job descriptions have safety specific responsibilities identified?
Is safety awareness communicated via activities such as newsletters, alerts, and toolbox talks, etc.?
Are applicable site safety rules and/or management processes provided to contractor?
Is there a written Contractor Safety Program?
Are safety control measures determined for the contractor's services reviewed with BS in advance?
Is on site check performed before starting the contractor's services to ensure that the determined control measures are in place?
Are there established policies/procedures describing safety expectations, roles, and responsibilities?
Are risk assessments completed and communicated with teammates to identify job hazards?
Are corrective actions, with accountability, tracked to closure?
Are controls selected according to the “hierarchy of controls,” emphasizing, in order of priority; elimination, substitution, engineering controls, administrative controls, and PPE?
Has a standard practice for Vehicle Elevating and Lowering Operation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
Has a standard practice for Tire Inflation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
Has a standard practice for Tire/Large Tire Manual Handling Operation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
Has a standard practice for Motor Vehicle Safety that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
Have controls been developed and implemented to prevent Mechanical Hazard risks?
Is there a documented process for investigation and corrective action following a safety incident?
Once safety controls installed, are they monitored to ensure that teammates understand their use and application and to verify that they are effective?
Are interim controls adopted while permanent controls are being determined?
Before making changes to operations, workflow/work methodology, physical plant/facility, equipment, or materials, is there a safety review to identify any safety or health issues?
Do teammates receive supplemental training when a change in the workplace could introduce new or increased hazards?
Are leading indicators used to review and analyze safety policies, procedures and trends for continual improvement?
Are performance indicators used to track progress toward safety program goals?
Are targeted safety campaigns/initiatives used to improve performance?
